payload.title: NYCT 2025 DOS Agency Safety Plan
payload.publisher: New York City Transit (NYCT) – Office of System Safety
payload.description: 2025 Agency Safety Plan (ASP) for New York City Transit Department of Subways, version 7, covering sections 1-9 as an annual update including safety performance targets, safety management policy, safety risk management, safety assurance, and safety promotion.

tool_args.label: Get It Fixed Reporting Website Added
tool_args.raw_text: An additional online reporting mechanism called \"Get It Fixed\" located at https://mta.info/getitfixed , has been established. Get It Fixed is an online reporting portal developed for use by NYCT employees to report non-emergency issues like customer behavior, equipment issues, climate/comfort, and cleanliness among other reportable topics.

payload.claim_text: Get It Fixed is an online reporting portal developed for use by NYCT employees to report non-emergency issues like customer behavior, equipment issues, climate/comfort, and cleanliness among other reportable topics.
payload.description: Section 4.6.3 Get It Fixed Reporting Website added to the 2025 ASP at page 4-19

evidence_refs.source_quote: Pg 4-19 Added Section 4.6.3 Get It Fixed Reporting Website – "An additional online reporting mechanism called "Get It Fixed" located at https://mta.info/getitfixed , has been established. Get It Fixed is an online reporting portal developed for use by NYCT employees to report non-emergency issues like customer behavior, equipment issues, climate/comfort, and cleanliness among other reportable topics."

tool_args.raw_text: The DOS Safety, Safety Hazards and Risk Prevention program (SHARP) a proactive program using data from employee referrals, field observations, audits, and injury data to identify and address potential safety trends.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Pg 5-3: Section 5.1.1 Hazard Identification added, "The DOS Safety, Safety Hazards and Risk Prevention program (SHARP) a proactive program using data from employee referrals, field observations, audits, and injury data to identify and address potential safety trends."

tool_args.label: OSS Near-Miss Committee Established
tool_args.raw_text: OSS has established near-miss committee to review and conduct quarterly trend analyses on both potential for employee contact (PEC) and non-PEC near-miss incidents.

payload.claim_text: OSS has established near-miss committee to review and conduct quarterly trend analyses on both potential for employee contact (PEC) and non-PEC near-miss incidents. The committee consists of the Chief Officer of OSS - Operations, the Chief Officer of OSS - Hazard Assessment, the Manager of OSS - Rapid Transit Investigations, and the Manager of OSS - Hazard Analysis.
payload.description: Section 6.3.3A OSS Investigation of Near Miss Incidents updated to include established near-miss committee

evidence_refs.source_quote: Pg 6-140: Section 6.3.3A OSS Investigation of Near Miss Incidents, updated to include, " Additionally, OSS has established near-miss committee to review and conduct quarterly trend analyses on both potential for employee contact (PEC) and non-PEC near-miss incidents.

tool_args.label: De-Escalation and Workplace Violence Training for DOS
tool_args.raw_text: A standalone de-escalation training has been established for Department of Subways employees.

payload.claim_text: A standalone de-escalation training has been established for Department of Subways employees. In the training techniques are provided enabling employees to recognize and identify situations which can potentially put themselves at risk as well as handling confrontational interactions.
payload.description: Section 7.1.4 De-Escalation and Workplace Violence Training Updated

evidence_refs.source_quote: Pg 7-15: Section 7.1.4 De-Escalation and Workplace Violence Training Updated to include "A standalone de-escalation training has been established for Department of Subways employees.
